rationale.R1.2: The agent provides a detailed analysis of the JudgeMT protocol and identifies one consistency issue with a localized fix (self-preference argument gap).
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (presenting JudgeMT as a drop-in replacement for human DA).
rationale.RX.3: The output covers substantially different active set than requested (generalizing the framework beyond the tested conditions).
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (positioning an LLM judge as a human-evaluation substitute can entrench stylistic preferences).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason.
